Nature of Content / Typical Use Case: Introduces the core topic, subtitle, date, and presenter.
Outlines the roadmap of topics to set audience expectations.
Delivers the "TL;DR" or a high-level briefing of the entire deck.
Highlights a specific pain point or market gap to establish context.
Concisely states the core benefit or solution being offered.
Articulates the overarching purpose and future goals of an organization.
Establishes credibility with a headshot, title, and brief background.
Signals a shift to a new chapter or major theme to maintain flow.
The standard workhorse for delivering structured, text-based points.
Compares two distinct ideas, splits long lists, or balances text.
Highlights three core features, strategic pillars, or distinct services.
Isolates a single, powerful statistic to ensure maximum audience retention.
Emphasizes a testimonial, expert quote, or critical guiding principle.
Presents the main narrative alongside a narrow margin for definitions or notes.
Distills complex sections into 3-4 bulleted conclusions.
Displays primary quantitative trends, growth, or data distributions.
Consolidates several related charts for a holistic performance view.
Organizes dense numerical data or comparative text into rows/columns.
Visualizes cumulative financial impacts, such as P&L or cash flow.
Maps the correlation or distribution between variables across axes.
Outlines high-level income statements, revenue, or EBITDA projections.
Displays Key Performance Indicators in a clean, easily scannable grid.
Illustrates conversion rates, sales pipelines, or user drop-off stages.
Highlights a central percentage surrounded by its constituent parts.
Leverages an edge-to-edge background photo to evoke emotion or scale.
Pairs an image directly with a contextual paragraph or explanation.
Showcases a portfolio, product line, or multiple locations simultaneously.
Embeds playback media to demonstrate a product or show an interview.
Replaces heavy text with simple symbols to quickly communicate features.
Displays software, apps, or UI designs framed inside a laptop or phone.
Maps out technical infrastructure, data pipelines, or software stacks.
Illustrates the step-by-step path a user takes through an interface.
Visually contrasts a baseline state against the post-intervention result.
Tracks chronological events, company history, or phases left-to-right.
Tracks sequential milestones reading from top-to-bottom.
Details linear workflows, onboarding processes, or specific instructions.
Illustrates continuous, repeating workflows (e.g., Agile methodology).
Maps complex project schedules, task dependencies, and durations.
Visualizes future feature releases and strategic goals over quarters/years.
Maps workflows while specifying which department/role owns each task.
A 4-quadrant grid evaluating Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, Threats.
Positions a brand against competitors based on two defining axes.
Details the demographics, behaviors, and pain points of an ideal user.
Compares SaaS subscription levels, packages, and included features.

Details the launch plan, marketing channels, and sales approach.
Visualizes corporate hierarchy, reporting structures, and departments.

Shows market penetration, office locations, or logistical routes.
